critical_updates.details: Client is ahead of schedule on fine payments, having made a payment last Friday. Remaining balance is $1,200. Client has also completed 25 out of 50 required community service hours.

Client continues to work 40 hours a week at a temp agency performing data entry. They are currently awaiting a ruling from the state board on their real estate license appeal, expected in 30 to 45 days.
Client is currently able to cover their mortgage but expressed concern about depleting savings in four months and potentially needing to list their condo if the real estate license appeal is denied.
Client is planning to travel out of state to attend their sister's baby shower, for which an official travel pass was approved by the supervisor.

next_episode_context: Client is maintaining stability but facing financial and professional stress. They are currently working 40 hours a week at a temp agency (data entry) to pay their mortgage while awaiting a state board ruling on their real estate license appeal (expected within 30-45 days). If denied, they may need to sell their condo. Client is ahead on court fines with a balance of $1,200 and has completed exactly half (25/50) of their community service hours, with 4 more planned at a food bank this weekend. They successfully obtained a travel pass for a family baby shower out-of-state. The next supervision check-in is scheduled for Tuesday the 14th at 9:00 AM.
